story.title: ONVIF camera discovery: find cameras on the LAN and auto-populate the Camera Builder with names, stream profiles, and RTSP URLs
story.as_a: Frigate user who doesn't know (or want to Google) their camera's RTSP URL pattern
story.i_want: Dinghy to discover cameras on my network, and after I give per-camera credentials, fill the Camera Builder with the exact RTSP URLs and stream profiles the camera reports
story.so_that: the 'find the magic URL' step disappears entirely

acceptance_criteria: Given the desktop app on a LAN, when the user clicks 'Discover Cameras (ONVIF)' in the Camera Builder, then a WS-Discovery probe (UDP multicast 239.255.255.250:3702) runs with a 4s window and discovered devices list with name/hardware (from probe scopes) and address — completing in under 5 seconds

Given a discovered device, when the user enters username/password and clicks Connect, then Dinghy queries GetDeviceInformation and the device's media profiles, and lists each profile with name, resolution, and frame rate

Given a listed profile, when the user clicks 'Use', then the Camera Builder form is populated: camera name slugified from model/name (passing the app's name regex), Input Path set to the profile's RTSP URI with credentials embedded (Frigate URL format), detect width/height/fps from the profile, and roles pre-checked detect (+record when it is the highest-resolution profile)
Given no devices respond or UDP multicast is blocked, when discovery completes, then a friendly inline message explains nothing was found and manual entry remains fully usable
Given wrong credentials or an unreachable device, when Connect runs, then a structured error shows inline (credentials never echoed) and the device stays in the list for retry
Given dinghy.html opened in a plain browser, when the Camera Builder renders, then no ONVIF UI exists at all (guarded like all native features)
constraints: Library: agsh/onvif (npm 'onvif') in the main process only; discovery and device queries never run in the renderer

IPC surface additions: onvif:discover → [{address, port, name, hardware, xaddr}]; onvif:device (address, port, username, password) → {manufacturer, model, profiles:[{name, token, width, height, fps, rtsp}]} | {error}

Credentials are passed per-call over IPC, held only in memory for the call, never logged, never written to trace, and stripped from any error text; the only persistence is the user-visible RTSP URL in the camera input (identical to typing it manually)
Scope per master spec: discovery only — no PTZ, no analytics, no device configuration; non-ONVIF cameras fall back to manual entry
Discovery results are transient native-only UI state (module-level, not Store) — documented deviation from the Store-only rule since results are never persisted or rendered by View
All renderer additions guarded by window.dinghyNative; zero impact on browser build
edge_cases: Device responds to probe but rejects unauthenticated GetDeviceInformation — name falls back to probe-scope name; profiles still require creds (normal flow)
RTSP URI from GetStreamUri already contains a port or query string — credentials injected after scheme without disturbing the rest
Multiple network interfaces — onvif lib probes default interface; document limitation
Duplicate discovery clicks — button disabled while a probe is in flight
Camera reports profile without videoEncoderConfiguration — profile listed with unknown resolution, still usable
Slugified name collides with existing camera — existing Validate.cameraName surfaces the inline error on Add as usual
macOS local-network permission prompt may appear on first probe — note in README
dependencies: UDP multicast reachable on user LAN
risks: Medium: ONVIF implementations vary wildly across vendors — defensive parsing with optional chaining, profile-level fallbacks
Medium: cannot integration-test against real cameras in this environment — QA covers syntax/IPC contract/browser parity/launch smoke; real-camera validation is Human T5
Low: agsh/onvif callback-style API quirks — wrapped in promisified helpers

open_questions.question: Where do discovered-camera credentials live after Connect?

options.label: Only in the generated RTSP URL (Recommended)
options.info: Matches manual-entry behavior and Frigate's own config format; no new secret store.

options.label: Electron safeStorage keychain
options.info: More secure at rest but adds a secrets subsystem v0.4 doesn't otherwise need.

open_questions.selected: Only in the generated RTSP URL (Recommended)

tasks.description: Add 'onvif' dependency; main.js IPC: onvif:discover (Discovery.probe, scope parsing for name/hardware, address extraction from xaddrs) and onvif:device (Cam connect, getDeviceInformation, profiles, getStreamUri per profile, credential-sanitized errors)
preload.js: discoverCameras(), getCameraStreams(params) added to dinghyNative
Camera Builder UI (native-only): Discover button, device list with per-device creds + Connect, profile rows with Use buttons populating the form (slugified name, creds-embedded RTSP, dims/fps, roles)
QA: node --check all surfaces, browser parity (no ONVIF UI, v0.2/v0.3 regressions), Electron launch smoke, scope-parsing unit check via node, credential-leak grep of error paths
Human: discover real cameras on home LAN, connect with creds, verify populated RTSP works in Frigate; confirm <5s discovery and macOS local-network permission flow

docs_md:
## Dinghy v0.4 — ONVIF Discovery
Desktop-only. 'Discover Cameras (ONVIF)' sends a WS-Discovery multicast probe; responding cameras list with their advertised name and address. Enter that camera's credentials and Connect to pull its real stream profiles; one click drops a profile into the Camera Builder form with the exact RTSP URL. Discovery only — no PTZ or device configuration. Non-ONVIF cameras: manual entry as before.
metrics: Discovery round-trip (click → list rendered) ≤ 5s with 4s probe window
0 occurrences of password values in any error string or trace output (code-path review + grep)
Browser build: 0 ONVIF DOM nodes rendered, 0 regressions in parity suite
Real-camera validation (T5): populated RTSP URL accepted by Frigate unmodified

decision_log.rationale: Two-stage UX (unauthenticated probe → per-device creds → profiles) reflects how ONVIF actually works rather than pretending discovery alone yields stream URLs. Main-process-only ONVIF keeps the renderer sandbox intact and the bridge auditably small.
alternatives_considered: Rejected: ask for one global credential and try it on every device — leaks creds to every responder, fails mixed-vendor setups
Rejected: raw hand-rolled WS-Discovery/SOAP — agsh/onvif is the master-spec-named, maintained path
Rejected: storing creds in a keychain (oq_001) — new subsystem with no v0.4 payoff
tradeoffs: Discovery panel state lives outside Store (documented)
Real-camera correctness rests on Human T5
decision_log.confidence_rationale: IPC and UI are straightforward; vendor variance in ONVIF responses is the known unknown, mitigated by defensive parsing and per-profile fallbacks.

defaults_applied: Assuming creds live only in the generated RTSP URL (oq_001 recommended)
Assuming 'record' role pre-checks only on the highest-resolution profile; 'detect' pre-checks on all
Assuming discovery UI lives in the Camera Builder form card above manual fields
Assuming onvif lib default network interface behavior is acceptable for v0.4
